summary: EXP-410 closes ISS-1965 (SEC-6.14.c1 H2 [moderate], decider class_hint=deep-attack). Inserts a single new paragraph in SEC-6.14 "What the dome could do" subsection between the bedrock paragraph and the OTS canary paragraph, dismantling the Chebychev-low-pass rescue on its own technical terms with three independent kill-points: (1) the published Membach SG-null analysis (Van Camp et al. 2001, DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637) works from un-decimated 1-Hz raw records, not Level-3 publication residuals — the rescue’s target data product is not the data product the null was extracted from; (2) IGETS/GGP Level-1 SG archive at GFZ Potsdam holds the 1-Hz raw record publicly, so the dome must explain why every Level-1 reanalysis missed a 30–900×-above-noise feature and why the dome itself declines to perform the reanalysis the rescue invites; (3) the rescue is self-defeating against κ’s numerator — the same era pipeline produced the Mohe 6.5 µGal denominator, so if Chebychev pre-decimation band-stops the predicted signal out of Membach 1999 and Chinese 2009 records, the same filters band-stopped the Mohe "valleys" out of κ’s denominator, making the Mohe number a pipeline artifact and Yang & Wang 2002’s reanalysis the correct reading. 467 words added (doubles the subsection length, proportionate to deep-attack scope and operator’s explicit class_hint). Locator unique in part6.html and entire sections.json. No DATA blob, layer count, or classifier touched. patch_type=targeted-paragraph-insertion; decider can apply via standard find-and-replace. review_class=deep-attack — curmudgeon should re-review on technical specifics (Van Camp 2001 raw-record analysis claim; IGETS archive cite; Mohe self-defeat fork), not just verifying prose landed.
renumbered_reason: ID collision detected mid-run with EXP-409-ISS-1747-cross-convention-xref.json (pushed to origin/main at 01:05Z after my clone). My Chebychev EXP renumbered EXP-409 → EXP-410.
